title: Compilation issues with PLI and DPI on MSVC
body:
---


Author Name: **Amir Gonnen**
Original Redmine Issue: 571 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2012-10-22


---

I'm compiling a verilated module on MSVC 10 (Windows 7, Verilator-3.820)

Since I'm compiling it as a lib, I don't need it to export any symbol (I call the PLI/DPI from my C++ wrapper which is compiled in the same application)

I've encountered the following issues:

1. DPI functions (on svdpi.h) are always exported through XXTERN which is eventually defined as __declspec(dllimport). When not compiling to DLL, this results in linking errors. To work around this I had to comment the definition of DPI_DLLISPEC on svdpi.h and define it as nothing.
1. Same problem with XXTERN on vpi_user.h and PLI_DLLISPEC/PLI_DLLESPEC. In that case since the definition of PLI_DLLISPEC/PLI_DLLESPEC is guarded by ifndef, it's possible to define it externally and avoid changing vpi_user.h file itself.
1. On verilated_vpi.h, the vpi_register_cb uses the VL_TIME_Q macro. This macro is defined as a call to sc_time_stamp (on verilated.h) and declares an extern if needed. When not compiling with systemc I get an "unresolved symbol sc_time_stamp" error on linking. To work around this I had to declare the sc_time_stamp function myself, but I'm not sure yet how to calculate the time returned there... meanwhile I call vl_fatal.

Thanks,

Amir
